join_order.probe: orders(zone_map_pruned + o_orderdate < 1995-03-15, ~7.5M candidate rows → ~3.75M after custkey filter)
lineitem(l_shipdate > 1995-03-15, ~30M rows scanned from sorted midpoint)

filter_pushdown.note: dictionary-encoded; lookup BUILDING code once, compare integers
orders sorted by o_orderdate; zone map prunes ~50% of blocks (those fully after cutoff)
bitset probe eliminates non-BUILDING customers; no hash lookup overhead
lineitem sorted by l_shipdate; binary-search for cutoff offset, scan only latter ~50%

data_structures.customer_filter: bitset(1500001) — dense c_custkey domain [1..1500000], 187KB, fits in L2 cache; O(1) probe with no hash computation
data_structures.orders_join_build: compact_hash_map(pre_sized, power-of-2 slots ~8388608) on o_orderkey → {o_orderdate: int32_t, o_shippriority: int32_t}; multiply-shift hash, open addressing, load factor ~0.45
data_structures.aggregation: partitioned_hash_map(32 partitions) keyed by (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority) → int64_t revenue_sum; thread-local per-partition tables merged at end; ~3.75M groups estimated
data_structures.topk: min-heap of size 10 (priority_queue) for ORDER BY revenue DESC, o_orderdate LIMIT 10; O(N log 10)

parallelism.phase_1_customer_scan: parallel scan over 15 blocks (1.5M rows / 100K block), filter mktsegment, write to shared bitset using atomic OR on 64-bit words (race-free since each bit is set monotonically)
parallelism.phase_2_orders_scan: parallel scan over zone-map-pruned order blocks; each thread probes shared bitset (read-only after phase 1) and date filter; threads write matching rows to thread-local vectors, then parallel-build into shared compact_hash_map using partitioned insert (no locks: partition by o_orderkey hash, each partition owned by one thread)
parallelism.phase_3_lineitem_scan: parallel scan from binary-search cutoff offset to end of lineitem (sorted by l_shipdate); each thread probes orders compact_hash_map (read-only), accumulates into thread-local partitioned aggregation tables
parallelism.phase_4_merge_aggregate: sequential merge of 64 thread-local partitioned aggregation tables into global result (or partition-parallel merge: thread i merges partition i across all local tables)
parallelism.phase_5_topk: parallel local TopK(10) per thread over merged partitions, then final merge of 64×10 candidates
parallelism.synchronization: partitioned_no_contention — bitset phase uses atomic word writes; hash map build uses partition ownership; aggregation uses thread-local tables

index_usage.purpose: skip order blocks where max(o_orderdate) >= cutoff_epoch; orders sorted by o_orderdate means all blocks past the cutoff can be entirely skipped — expected ~50% block reduction
index_usage.layout: 150 blocks × {int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t count}; skip block if min > cutoff_epoch (1995-03-15 = 9204 days since 1970-01-01)

index_usage.purpose: NOT used as primary probe structure — scanning lineitem filtered by l_shipdate is more efficient since lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate (50% rows can be skipped via binary search on the sorted column). The pre-built index would require random-access reads across all 60M rows.
index_usage.note: Considered but rejected: probing 3.75M o_orderkeys into hash_l_orderkey would require reading ~3.75M sets of positions from the positions array, each potentially scattered — worse memory access pattern than sequential lineitem scan with filter

index_usage.purpose: NOT used — scanning orders with zone_map pruning + bitset probe is more cache-friendly than random-access lookups for 300K customer keys into hash_o_custkey (would cause ~300K random position reads scattered across 15M order rows)
index_usage.note: Considered but rejected: iterating 300K BUILDING custkeys through hash_o_custkey yields ~2M scattered order row positions; sequential scan with bitset is more cache-efficient at this scale

scan_details.customer: full scan (1.5M rows, 15 blocks); fused filter on dictionary c_mktsegment code for 'BUILDING'; write matching c_custkey to bitset
scan_details.orders: zone-map-pruned scan; binary-search blocks using zone_map_o_orderdate to find first block where max >= cutoff, skip all blocks where min > cutoff (sorted data: contiguous range of valid blocks); fused dual-filter: o_orderdate < cutoff AND bitset[o_custkey]
scan_details.lineitem: sorted-data early-start scan: binary search on l_shipdate column to find first row index where l_shipdate > cutoff (days_since_epoch 9204); scan from that offset to end; fused probe into orders hash map

physical_plan.memory_pattern: sequential for all scans; bitset probe = L2-resident random access (187KB); orders hash map probe = L3-resident random access (~3.75M × 8B key+value = ~30-60MB, fits in 44MB L3 with care — partition to ensure each thread's working set fits)

late_materialization.reason: all needed columns (c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority, l_orderkey, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_shipdate) are integer/decimal types; no string materialization needed at join time (c_mktsegment is decoded to integer code once)

decimal_handling.revenue_formula: l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) / 100  [all int64_t, scale_factor=2; multiply then adjust scale]
decimal_handling.note: l_extendedprice and l_discount both scale_factor=2; (1 - l_discount) in scaled form = (100 - l_discount); revenue = l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) in scale_factor=4 intermediate, store as int64_t sum

estimated_cost.bottleneck: lineitem scan (~30M rows post-filter) with hash map probes; hash map for orders (~3.75M entries, ~45MB) may exceed L3 cache (44MB) — partitioned approach mitigates by keeping each thread's active partition in cache
estimated_cost.target_vs_umbra: Umbra achieves 64ms; targeting 80-120ms with 64-thread parallelism, zone-map pruning, bitset semi-join reduction, and single-pass fused scans
